skill.name: prompt-engineering
skill.description: Master prompt engineering for AI models: LLMs, image generators, video models. Techniques: chain-of-thought, few-shot, system prompts, negative prompts. Models: Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, FLUX, Veo, Stable Diffusion prompting. Use for: better AI outputs, consistent results, complex tasks, optimization. Triggers: prompt engineering, how to prompt, better prompts, prompt tips, prompting guide, llm prompting, image prompt, ai prompting, prompt optimization, prompt template, prompt structure, effective prompts, prompt techniques
skill.summary: Master prompt engineering for LLMs, image generators, and video models with techniques like chain-of-thought, few-shot learning, and system prompts.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation guide for prompt engineering techniques. The static scanner flagged numerous false positives: bash code blocks in markdown were misidentified as shell execution, URLs in documentation were flagged as network calls, and words containing 'crypt' triggered crypto algorithm warnings. The only legitimate concern is the curl|sh pattern on line 16, which is a standard CLI installation method with documented checksum verification. No malicious code or exfiltration patterns found.

medium_findings.title: Shell Installation Pattern
medium_findings.description: Line 16 contains 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' - a pipe-to-shell pattern commonly used for CLI installation. While this is a standard pattern, users should be aware. The skill documents SHA-256 checksum verification as a security measure.

content.user_title: Master Prompt Engineering for AI Models
content.value_statement: Learn proven techniques to write better prompts for LLMs, image generators, and video models to get more accurate and useful outputs.
actual_capabilities: Write effective prompts for large language models using role prompting, task clarity, and constraint setting
Create detailed prompts for image generation models like FLUX and Stable Diffusion with subject, style, and composition guidance
Apply chain-of-thought and few-shot learning techniques to improve model reasoning and output quality
Structure prompts for video generation models with camera movements, temporal keywords, and action descriptions
Specify output formats and use system prompts to control model behavior for consistent results
Apply model-specific tips for Claude, GPT-4, FLUX, and Veo to optimize prompting strategies
limitations: Does not execute code or run AI models directly - it is a guide teaching prompting techniques
Effectiveness depends on the underlying model capabilities and version
Does not provide real-time API access or model hosting
Some techniques may not work equally well across all AI model types

use_cases.title: Improve LLM Output Quality
use_cases.description: Learn to write clearer, more effective prompts for code generation, content writing, and analysis tasks using role prompting, chain-of-thought, and output format specifications.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and content creators using Claude, GPT-4, or similar LLMs

use_cases.title: Create Better Image Prompts
use_cases.description: Master the art of describing subjects, styles, lighting, and composition for FLUX, Stable Diffusion, and other image generation models to get the exact images you envision.
use_cases.target_user: Digital artists, designers, and creative professionals using AI image generators

use_cases.title: Generate Consistent Results
use_cases.description: Use system prompts, structured output specifications, and iterative refinement techniques to get reproducible, consistent outputs across multiple prompt iterations.
use_cases.target_user: AI enthusiasts and professionals who need reliable, repeatable AI outputs

prompt_templates.title: Basic LLM Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: You are a [role]. [Task description with specific details]. [Constraints or requirements]. Output format: [specified format].
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need structured, role-aware responses from LLMs

prompt_templates.title: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
prompt_templates.prompt: Solve this step by step: [problem]. Think through each step before giving the final answer. Show your reasoning process.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need detailed analytical responses or math/logic solutions

prompt_templates.title: Few-Shot Learning Template
prompt_templates.prompt:
Complete the following [task type]:

Example 1:
Input: [example input 1]
Output: [example output 1]

Example 2:
Input: [example input 2]
Output: [example output 2]

Now complete:
Input: [your input]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need specific output formats or styles demonstrated through examples

prompt_templates.title: Image Generation Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: [Subject with specific details], [setting/background], [lighting type], [art style or photography style], [composition], [quality keywords like photorealistic, 8K, ultra detailed]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when generating images with FLUX, Stable Diffusion, or similar models

output_examples.output: Vague prompt → unpredictable results. The skill teaches you to specify: language, exact problem, expected behavior, and required output format for better responses.
Basic request → generic output. The skill shows how to add: genre, tone, target audience, length, key plot points, and specific narrative constraints for tailored results.

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a cat
output_examples.output: Simple prompt → basic image. The skill demonstrates: detailed subject description (fluffy orange tabby with green eyes), style (vintage portrait), lighting (soft natural), composition (rule of thirds), and quality keywords (photorealistic, 8K).

best_practices: Be specific and include relevant details - vague prompts produce unpredictable results
Use role prompting to establish context and expertise level for the AI
Specify output format explicitly when you need structured, machine-readable results
anti_patterns: Being too vague - prompts like 'help me' or 'make it better' produce inconsistent results
Including conflicting constraints that confuse the model about priorities
Not specifying length limits, causing outputs to be too long or too short

faq.question: What is prompt engineering?
faq.answer: Prompt engineering is the practice of crafting effective inputs (prompts) for AI models to get better, more accurate outputs. It involves structuring requests with clear roles, tasks, constraints, and output specifications.

faq.question: Which AI models work with these techniques?
faq.answer: These techniques work with most AI models including Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, FLUX, Stable Diffusion, and Veo. Each model may respond slightly differently to certain techniques.

faq.question: What is chain-of-thought prompting?
faq.answer: Chain-of-thought prompting encourages the AI to show its reasoning process step by step before providing the final answer. This improves accuracy on complex logic and math problems.

faq.question: How do I get consistent outputs?
faq.answer: Use system prompts to set persistent behavior, specify exact output formats (JSON, bullet points), include few-shot examples, and set clear length and constraint guidelines.

faq.question: What are negative prompts?
faq.answer: Negative prompts are instructions telling the AI what to avoid in the output. They are commonly used in image generation to exclude unwanted elements like blur, watermarks, or specific styles.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, the prompting techniques in this skill apply to Claude, Claude Code, Codex, and other LLM-based AI assistants. The principles of role prompting, clarity, and format specification are universal.
